structural_claim: Collective phenomena emerge from quantum many-body systems through the selection of preferred modes that break underlying symmetries

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Environmental entanglement dynamically selects preferred pointer states, explaining the emergence of classical behavior
Classical objectivity emerges through redundant encoding of selected information in the environment
All nuclei exhibit a collective oscillation mode where protons and neutrons oscillate against each other, demonstrating that nuclei behave as quantum fluids with collective degrees of freedom
The QCD vacuum spontaneously breaks chiral symmetry, generating constituent quark masses
